Installed capacity of the unit, in megawatts
Current status of plant, see definitions in About tab

value_definitions.name: announced
value_definitions.definition: Projects that have been publicly reported but have not yet moved actively forward by applying for permits or seeking land, material, or financing.

value_definitions.name: pre-construction
value_definitions.definition: Projects that are actively moving forward in seeking governmental approvals, land rights, or financing.

value_definitions.name: construction
value_definitions.definition: Site preparation and other activities are underway.

value_definitions.name: operating
value_definitions.definition: The plant has been formally commissioned; commercial operation has begun.

value_definitions.name: shelved
value_definitions.definition: Two years have passed with no action on a project after the project proposal.

value_definitions.name: cancelled
value_definitions.definition: A cancellation announcement has been made. The status is marked as ‘cancelled – inferred 4 y’ if no progress has been observed for at least four years.

value_definitions.name: mothballed
value_definitions.definition: The project is disused, but not dismantled.

value_definitions.name: retired
value_definitions.definition: The project has been decommissioned; this term is also used if the plant has been destroyed by war.

fields.definition_from_readme: Equipment technology. Options include: internal combustion, combined cycle, gas turbine, internal combustion combined cycle, integrated solar combined cycle, steam turbine, Allam-Fetvedt Cycle
Equpment manufacturer and model.
Combined heat and power/cogeneration. Options include: yes, no, not found

Hydrogen capable: turbine - The turbine at the gas plant is capable of burning some % of hydrogen. 
Hydrogen capable: progress - The gas plant has made some sort of progress beyond having a gas turbine that is hydrogen capable. This could include partnerships to secure hydrogen, announcements towards hydrogen infrastructure, or an actual hydrogen contract.
Using hydrogen - The plant is using hydrogen as a fuel.
N - The plant is not capable of using hydrogen. 
not found - no information found
Carbon capture and storage (CCS) is most relevant for in development projects. Options include: yes, no, not found

Options include: 
conversion - converts the steam turbine of an existing plant to burn gas.
replacement - existing plant is retired and replaced with a new  gas-fired plant. 
change of plans - cases in which one or more  units were planned and those plans were cancelled and subsequently gas units were planned at the same location.
use of ST in CC set - cases where there is a steam turbine (usually coal) and is then repurposed to be part of a gas-fired combined cycle unit. Can also be referred to as “repowering” into a combined cycle where gas turbine(s) and HSRGs are added to the existing steam turbine. Was labeled as "partial conversion" in previous GOGPT data releases
not found

top_values: coal: unknown, fossil liquids: petroleum coke

Year for the unit's commissioning; For combined cycle sets, which are composed of multiple gas and steam turbines which may not have all been built at the same time, the start year reflects when they began combined cycle operation together.
Year the plant is decommissioned
Future date for the unit's planned retirement

fields.definition_from_readme: For example, government database ID

"Captive" power plants are those that are within industrial facilities. These power plants typically supply most of their power (and if they're CHP, most of their heat as well) to the industrial facility. Options include: heat, power, both.

The type of captive industry use. Options include: agriculture, aluminum, automobiles, cement & building, chemicals, coal mining & coal products, cryptocurrency, dairy, data center, electronics, hydrogen production, industrial park, iron & steel, machinery, nickel, nutrition, oil & refining, other, other metals & mining, pulp & paper, rubber, sugar, textiles, unknown
Indicate if heat or power is used outside of the captive industrial facility or complex. Options include: heat, power, both, none. 'None' indicates an entirely captive plant.
Internal generated ID for the power station (non-unique)
Internal generated ID for the unit (unique)
Fuel classification options include: Oil only, LNG only, Gas only, Multi fuel.   Note "Gas only" does not include LNG.
